evals.name: fresh-scaffold-dotnet
evals.prompt: I just spun up a new ASP.NET Core Web API called OrderService (net8.0, has a .sln + one csproj under src/OrderService, plain git init, no CLAUDE.md yet). Before the team starts building on it I want the whole AI-dev constitution set up — rules, OKF docs, backlog, the works.
evals.expected_output: Fresh-scaffold mode: dotnet profile confirmed, all owned rules copied verbatim, manifest at current VERSION, every Step 4 artifact scaffolded with no unresolved placeholders (adr template carve-out excepted), nothing committed.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_fresh — derived from skill source at grade time

evals.name: legacy-migration
evals.prompt: This repo (InvoiceApi) already has a CLAUDE.md we wrote by hand a year ago — it's got some architecture constraints and our bl/NNN-short-description branch naming convention in it, but nothing else, no ADRs, no journal, no backlog. Bring it up to our standard AI-dev setup without losing what's already written in there.
evals.expected_output: Legacy-migration mode: CLAUDE.md rewritten around the @import block with all original repo-specific content preserved verbatim somewhere in the result (architecture constraints, branch convention), remaining artifacts scaffolded, manifest written, nothing committed.

evals.name: upgrade
evals.prompt: We changed the Legislator's core rules and bumped VERSION since this repo was last legislated. Re-run it here so the repo picks up the update.
evals.expected_output: Upgrade mode: profiles reused from manifest without re-asking, the rule added since last legislation is copied in, the retired rule is deleted (deletion propagation), manifest bumped to current VERSION with exact sorted ownedFiles, project-owned files (including CLAUDE.md) untouched, nothing committed. Fixture is generated by setup_workspace.py from the current skill source: it withholds the alphabetically last core rule and plants a retired-rule.md.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_upgrade — uses fixture_meta.json written at setup time

evals.name: idempotency
evals.prompt: (second run, same repo, after committing run 1's result) Re-run the legislator here — nothing about the constitution or the repo has changed.
evals.expected_output: Zero diff: git status and git diff HEAD both empty after the second run. This is the constitution's core promise and the cheapest regression signal for serialization/formatting drift.
